channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: ‘Beautiful blobs’: synthetic life a step closer as scientists make cells using lab-made DNA - The Guardian
item.description:
1. ‘Beautiful blobs’: synthetic life a step closer as scientists make cells using lab-made DNA  The Guardian
  2. This Cell Feeds, Grows and Reproduces. And It’s Manmade.  The New York Times
  3. For the First Time, a Cell Built From Scratch Grows and Divides  Quanta Magazine
  4. Scientists say they have built a basic component of life from scratch  CNN
  5. Synthetic biology researchers think they’ve made a cell. Is it alive?  statnews.com

item.title: NASA to launch rescue mission July 2 to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere - Space
item.description:
1. NASA to launch rescue mission July 2 to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere  Space

  3. This space telescope is falling. A robotic spacecraft may save it  Science News
  4. NASA will conduct daring rescue mission to reposition telescope sinking into Earth's atmosphere  FOX Weather
  5. NASA races to save aging Swift telescope from falling back to Earth with daring rescue mission  PBS

item.title: 66 billion trees have been planted in China's Great Green Wall — and they appear to be growing faster than natural forests - Live Science
item.description:
1. 66 billion trees have been planted in China's Great Green Wall — and they appear to be growing faster than natural forests  Live Science

  3. Digital drive supports planting of millions of trees  China Daily
  4. 🌳 China has planted 66 billion trees: the consequences on our atmosphere  Techno-Science.net
  5. China's 66 billion trees in deserts are absorbing more CO2 and growing faster than natural ones  WION

item.title: Curiosity, Perseverance and Promise? NASA May Send a Mars Rover to the Moon. - The New York Times
item.description:
1. Curiosity, Perseverance and Promise? NASA May Send a Mars Rover to the Moon.  The New York Times
  2. NASA may send a backup, nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon  Ars Technica
  3. A fully built, fully tested lunar rover named VIPER faced dismantlement for parts after its 2024 cancellation, and NASA is now considering sending a Mars engineering testbed that has spent its working life in a JPL rock yard to the Moon instead  Space Daily
  4. 'PROMISE' me the moon? NASA wants to send spare nuclear-powered Mars rover to the lunar surface  Space
  5. Nuclear Mars Rover Will Conquer Deadly Nights At NASA Moon Base  HotHardware

item.title: The unsettling possibility raised by quantum gravity is that time may not be a basic ingredient of reality. It may be something that emerges only inside the universe, from entanglement, entropy, and relationships between physical systems — while the deeper qu - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The unsettling possibility raised by quantum gravity is that time may not be a basic ingredient of reality. It may be something that emerges only inside the universe, from entanglement, entropy, and relationships between physical systems — while the deeper qu  Space Daily

  3. Time may pass even in a universe that doesn't change  IAI TV
  4. Time May Flow in Reverse—And Control the Universe Across Multiple Dimensions, Theories Claim  Popular Mechanics

item.title: Dusting Off 3I/ATLAS: Interstellar Travelers Must Clean their Windshields! - Avi Loeb – Medium
item.description:
1. Dusting Off 3I/ATLAS: Interstellar Travelers Must Clean their Windshields!  Avi Loeb – Medium
  2. In July 2025, the ATLAS sky survey in Chile spotted a faint comet racing through the solar system at roughly 130,000 miles per hour — and follow-up orbit calculations quickly showed it was not bound to the Sun at all, but had arrived from interstellar space.  Space Daily
  3. California scientists scanned 3I/ATLAS for alien tech, and found only Earth-based noise  Yahoo
  4. A Rare Comet Made History as the Third Known Interstellar Object to Fly Through Our Solar System. Studies Are Now Revealing the Mysterious Conditions in Which It Formed  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. 12 billion years old, this interstellar comet is older than our solar system  Phys.org

item.title: Earth reaches its farthest point from the Sun on July 6, in the middle of northern summer, and the backward timing is the clearest sign the seasons don't come from distance - Space Daily
item.description: Earth reaches its farthest point from the Sun on July 6, in the middle of northern summer, and the backward timing is the clearest sign the seasons don't come from distance  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists expected Pluto to be a frozen, ancient world scarred by billions of years of impacts. But when New Horizons finally arrived in 2015, it found something stranger: smooth young plains with almost no craters, signs of flowing nitrogen ice, possible ice vol - Space Daily
item.description: Scientists expected Pluto to be a frozen, ancient world scarred by billions of years of impacts. But when New Horizons finally arrived in 2015, it found something stranger: smooth young plains with almost no craters, signs of flowing nitrogen ice, possible ice vol  Space Daily

item.title: We think of Mount Everest as the ultimate mountain, but Olympus Mons on Mars rises nearly three times as high and spreads across a footprint roughly the size of Italy, making Earth’s tallest peak look almost local by comparison. - Space Daily

item.title: Sharks are roughly 450 million years old — older than trees, older than forests, older than the first broad leaves. When they first appeared, life on land was still mostly low, simple mats of early plants, and nothing had yet learned how to grow into a forest. - Space Daily
item.description: Sharks are roughly 450 million years old — older than trees, older than forests, older than the first broad leaves. When they first appeared, life on land was still mostly low, simple mats of early plants, and nothing had yet learned how to grow into a forest.  Space Daily

item.title: Carl Sagan spent years trying to persuade NASA to take a picture many saw as scientifically pointless: Earth from more than 6 billion kilometres away, reduced to a pale blue dot smaller than a pixel. Voyager 1 finally captured it in 1990 — and that tiny speck be - Space Daily
